parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Information security policy and topic-specific policies shall be defined, approved by management, published, communicated to and acknowledged by relevant personnel and relevant interested parties, and reviewed at planned intervals or if significant changes occur.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Create and publish an information security policy approved by management, stored in a version-controlled repository.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Store approved policies in S3 with versioning: aws s3api put-bucket-versioning --bucket <policy-bucket> --versioning-configuration Status=Enabled
- Upload policy: aws s3 cp info-security-policy.pdf s3://<policy-bucket>/policies/
- Use AWS Organizations SCPs to enforce baseline: aws organizations create-policy --name InfoSecBaseline --type SERVICE_CONTROL_POLICY --content file://scp.json

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Store policies in SharePoint or Azure Blob: az storage blob upload --container-name policies --file info-security-policy.pdf --name policies/info-security-policy.pdf

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify information security policies are defined and approved

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Information security roles and responsibilities shall be defined and allocated.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Define dedicated security roles and assign them to appropriate personnel using IAM roles and groups.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Create security admin role: aws iam create-role --role-name SecurityAdmin --assume-role-policy-document file://trust-policy.json
- Attach SecurityAudit policy: aws iam attach-role-policy --role-name SecurityAdmin --policy-arn arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/SecurityAudit
- Create security group: aws iam create-group --group-name SecurityTeam
- List current role assignments: aws iam list-roles --query 'Roles[?contains(RoleName,`Security`)]'

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Assign Security Admin role: az role assignment create --assignee <user-id> --role 'Security Admin' --scope /subscriptions/<sub-id>

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify security roles are defined in IAM and org structure

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Conflicting duties and conflicting areas of responsibility shall be segregated.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement segregation of duties using separate IAM roles for development, operations, and security functions.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Create separate roles for dev/ops/security: aws iam create-role --role-name DevRole --assume-role-policy-document file://dev-trust.json
- Add permission boundary: aws iam put-role-permissions-boundary --role-name DevRole --permissions-boundary arn:aws:iam::<account>:policy/DevBoundary
- Deny self-escalation: create policy with Deny on iam:CreateRole, iam:AttachRolePolicy for dev roles
- Use AWS Config rule iam-policy-no-statements-with-full-access to detect overly broad permissions

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Use Azure PIM for just-in-time access: az rest --method POST --uri /providers/Microsoft.Authorization/roleEligibilityScheduleRequests

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify segregation of duties is enforced through IAM policies

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Management shall require all personnel to apply information security in accordance with the established information security policy and topic-specific policies of the organization.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement automated policy compliance checks and require personnel acknowledgment.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Enable AWS Config conformance packs: aws configservice put-conformance-pack --conformance-pack-name OrgSecurityBaseline --template-body file://conformance-pack.yaml
- List compliance status: aws configservice describe-compliance-by-config-rule
- Enable Security Hub standards: aws securityhub batch-enable-standards --standards-subscription-requests StandardsArn=arn:aws:securityhub:::standards/aws-foundational-security-best-practices/v/1.0.0

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Assign Azure Policy initiative: az policy assignment create --name SecurityBaseline --policy-set-definition <initiative-id> --scope /subscriptions/<sub-id>

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify management enforces security policy compliance

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The organization shall establish and maintain contact with relevant authorities.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Document authority contacts and configure automated incident notification channels.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Configure alternate contacts: aws account put-alternate-contact --alternate-contact-type SECURITY --name 'Security Team' --email-address security@example.com --phone-number '+1-555-0100' --title 'CISO'
- Verify contacts: aws account get-alternate-contact --alternate-contact-type SECURITY
- Store authority contact list in SSM Parameter Store: aws ssm put-parameter --name /security/authority-contacts --value file://contacts.json --type String

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Set security contact: az security contact create --name default --email security@example.com --phone '+1-555-0100' --alert-notifications on

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify contacts with regulatory authorities are documented and accessible

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The organization shall establish and maintain contact with special interest groups or other specialist security forums and professional associations.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Subscribe to threat intelligence feeds and join relevant ISACs.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Enable AWS Security Hub integrations: aws securityhub enable-import-findings-for-product --product-arn arn:aws:securityhub:<region>::product/aws/inspector
- Subscribe to AWS Trusted Advisor: aws support describe-trusted-advisor-checks --language en
- Subscribe to SNS security bulletins: aws sns subscribe --topic-arn arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:aws-security-bulletins --protocol email --notification-endpoint security@example.com

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Enable threat intelligence in Sentinel: az sentinel threat-intelligence-indicator list --workspace-name <ws>

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify memberships in security information sharing groups

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Information relating to information security threats shall be collected and analysed to produce threat intelligence.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Enable GuardDuty for threat detection and integrate third-party threat intelligence feeds.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Enable GuardDuty: aws guardduty create-detector --enable --finding-publishing-frequency FIFTEEN_MINUTES
- Enable threat intel feeds: aws guardduty create-threat-intel-set --detector-id <id> --name CustomThreatIntel --format TXT --location s3://<bucket>/threat-list.txt --activate
- Enable GuardDuty Malware Protection: aws guardduty update-member-detectors --detector-id <id> --data-sources ScanEc2InstanceWithFindings={EbsVolumes=true}
- List findings: aws guardduty list-findings --detector-id <id> --finding-criteria '{"Criterion":{"severity":{"Gte":7}}}'

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Enable Microsoft Defender Threat Intelligence: az sentinel threat-intelligence-indicator list --workspace-name <ws>

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify threat intelligence collection and analysis is operational

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Information security shall be integrated into project management.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Integrate security checks into CI/CD pipelines and project workflows.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Add security scan stage in CodePipeline: aws codepipeline update-pipeline --pipeline file://pipeline-with-security-stage.json
- Enable CodeBuild with security scanning: aws codebuild create-project --name security-scan --source file://source.json --environment file://env.json
- Use Inspector for container scanning: aws inspector2 enable --resource-types ECR

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Add security scan task in Azure DevOps pipeline: az pipelines create --name security-gate --yaml-path security-pipeline.yml

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify security gates exist in project lifecycle

controls.title: Inventory of information and other associated assets

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: An inventory of information and other associated assets, including owners, shall be developed and maintained.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Use AWS Config and resource tagging to maintain a comprehensive asset inventory.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Enable AWS Config recording: aws configservice put-configuration-recorder --configuration-recorder name=default,roleARN=<role-arn> --recording-group allSupported=true
- Start recorder: aws configservice start-configuration-recorder --configuration-recorder-name default
- Query resources: aws configservice select-resource-config --expression "SELECT resourceId, resourceType, tags WHERE tags.key = 'Owner'"
- Tag resources with owner: aws ec2 create-tags --resources <resource-id> --tags Key=Owner,Value=<team> Key=Classification,Value=<level>
- Enable AWS Config rule required-tags: aws configservice put-config-rule --config-rule file://required-tags-rule.json

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose:
- List all resources: az resource list --query '[].{Name:name, Type:type, Tags:tags}' -o table
- Tag resources: az tag create --resource-id <id> --tags Owner=<team> Classification=<level>

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify a complete asset inventory is maintained with ownership

controls.title: Acceptable use of information and other associated assets

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Rules for the acceptable use of information and other associated assets shall be identified, documented and implemented.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement SCPs and resource policies to enforce acceptable use boundaries.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Create SCP denying unapproved regions: aws organizations create-policy --name DenyUnapprovedRegions --type SERVICE_CONTROL_POLICY --content '{"Version":"2012-10-17","Statement":[{"Effect":"Deny","Action":"*","Resource":"*","Condition":{"StringNotEquals":{"aws:RequestedRegion":["us-east-1","eu-west-1"]}}}]}'
- Attach SCP to OU: aws organizations attach-policy --policy-id <policy-id> --target-id <ou-id>
- Create Config rule for allowed instance types: aws configservice put-config-rule --config-rule file://allowed-instance-types.json

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Create Azure Policy for allowed locations: az policy assignment create --name AllowedLocations --policy '/providers/Microsoft.Authorization/policyDefinitions/e56962a6-4747-49cd-b67b-bf8b01975c4c' --params '{"listOfAllowedLocations":{"value":["eastus","westeurope"]}}'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify acceptable use policies are enforced through technical controls

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Personnel and other interested parties as appropriate shall return all the organization's assets in their possession upon change or termination of their employment, contract or agreement.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Automate access revocation on personnel departure using identity lifecycle management.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Delete user access keys: aws iam delete-access-key --user-name <user> --access-key-id <key-id>
- Deactivate MFA: aws iam deactivate-mfa-device --user-name <user> --serial-number <mfa-arn>
- Remove from all groups: aws iam list-groups-for-user --user-name <user> | jq -r '.Groups[].GroupName' | xargs -I {} aws iam remove-user-from-group --user-name <user> --group-name {}
- Delete login profile: aws iam delete-login-profile --user-name <user>
- Delete user: aws iam delete-user --user-name <user>

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose:
- Disable user: az ad user update --id <user-id> --account-enabled false
- Revoke sessions: az rest --method POST --uri /users/<user-id>/revokeSignInSessions

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify offboarding process includes asset return and access revocation

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Information shall be classified according to the information security needs of the organization based on confidentiality, integrity, availability and relevant interested party requirements.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement data classification using resource tags and Macie for automated discovery.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Enable Macie: aws macie2 enable-macie
- Create classification job: aws macie2 create-classification-job --job-type ONE_TIME --s3-job-definition '{"bucketDefinitions":[{"accountId":"<account-id>","buckets":["<bucket>"]}]}' --name DataClassification
- Tag resources with classification: aws s3api put-bucket-tagging --bucket <bucket> --tagging '{"TagSet":[{"Key":"DataClassification","Value":"Confidential"}]}'
- Create Config rule for required classification tag: aws configservice put-config-rule --config-rule file://require-classification-tag.json

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose:
- Create sensitivity labels: az security assessment create --name DataClassification
- Enable Azure Purview for data classification scanning

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify data classification scheme is implemented through tagging

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: An appropriate set of procedures for information labelling shall be developed and implemented in accordance with the information classification scheme adopted by the organization.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Enforce mandatory tagging for data classification labels on all resources.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Create tag policy: aws organizations create-policy --name RequireClassificationTag --type TAG_POLICY --content file://tag-policy.json
- Attach tag policy: aws organizations attach-policy --policy-id <policy-id> --target-id <root-id>
- Find untagged resources: aws resourcegroupstaggingapi get-resources --tag-filters Key=DataClassification --query 'ResourceTagMappingList[?Tags==`[]`]'
- Create Config rule required-tags: aws configservice put-config-rule --config-rule '{"ConfigRuleName":"required-classification","Source":{"Owner":"AWS","SourceIdentifier":"REQUIRED_TAGS"},"InputParameters":"{\"tag1Key\":\"DataClassification\"}"  }'

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Require tag policy: az policy assignment create --name RequireClassification --policy '/providers/Microsoft.Authorization/policyDefinitions/871b6d14-10aa-478d-b590-94f262ecfa99' --params '{"tagName":{"value":"DataClassification"}}'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify information labelling is consistently applied

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Information transfer rules, procedures, or agreements shall be in place for all types of transfer facilities within the organization and between the organization and other parties.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Enforce TLS for all data transfers and configure S3 bucket policies to deny unencrypted access.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Deny non-SSL S3 access: aws s3api put-bucket-policy --bucket <bucket> --policy '{"Version":"2012-10-17","Statement":[{"Effect":"Deny","Principal":"*","Action":"s3:*","Resource":["arn:aws:s3:::<bucket>","arn:aws:s3:::<bucket>/*"],"Condition":{"Bool":{"aws:SecureTransport":"false"}}}]}'
- Enforce TLS 1.2 minimum on ELBs: aws elbv2 modify-listener --listener-arn <arn> --ssl-policy ELBSecurityPolicy-TLS13-1-2-2021-06
- Enable VPC flow logs to monitor transfers: aws ec2 create-flow-logs --resource-type VPC --resource-ids <vpc-id> --traffic-type ALL --log-destination-type s3 --log-destination arn:aws:s3:::<bucket>

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Enforce HTTPS on storage: az storage account update --name <account> --min-tls-version TLS1_2 --https-only true

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify encryption in transit is enforced for all data transfers

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Rules to control physical and logical access to information and other associated assets shall be established and implemented based on business and information security requirements.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement role-based access control with least privilege principles.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Run IAM Access Analyzer: aws accessanalyzer create-analyzer --analyzer-name OrgAnalyzer --type ORGANIZATION
- List findings for over-permissive access: aws accessanalyzer list-findings --analyzer-arn <arn>
- Generate least-privilege policy: aws accessanalyzer start-policy-generation --policy-generation-details '{"principalArn":"<role-arn>"}' --cloud-trail-details '{"trailArn":"<trail-arn>","startTime":"2024-01-01","endTime":"2024-12-31"}'
- Enable IAM Access Analyzer unused access: aws accessanalyzer create-analyzer --analyzer-name UnusedAccess --type ORGANIZATION --configuration '{"unusedAccess":{"unusedAccessAge":90}}'

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Review access with Access Reviews: az rest --method POST --uri /identityGovernance/accessReviews/definitions --body @access-review.json

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify access control policies enforce least privilege

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The full life cycle of identities shall be managed.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement centralized identity management with SSO and lifecycle automation.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Enable IAM Identity Center (SSO): aws sso-admin create-instance
- Create permission set: aws sso-admin create-permission-set --instance-arn <arn> --name ReadOnlyAccess --session-duration PT8H
- List all users: aws identitystore list-users --identity-store-id <id>
- Check for stale credentials: aws iam generate-credential-report && aws iam get-credential-report --output text --query Content | base64 -d

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Configure Entra ID lifecycle workflows: az rest --method GET --uri /identityGovernance/lifecycleWorkflows/workflows

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify identity lifecycle management is in place

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Allocation and management of authentication information shall be controlled by a management process, including advising personnel on appropriate handling of authentication information.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Enforce strong password policies and MFA for all users.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Set password policy: aws iam update-account-password-policy --minimum-password-length 14 --require-symbols --require-numbers --require-uppercase-characters --require-lowercase-characters --max-password-age 90 --password-reuse-prevention 24
- Check MFA status: aws iam generate-credential-report && aws iam get-credential-report --output text --query Content | base64 -d | cut -d, -f1,4,8
- Create Config rule for MFA: aws configservice put-config-rule --config-rule '{"ConfigRuleName":"mfa-enabled-for-iam-console-access","Source":{"Owner":"AWS","SourceIdentifier":"MFA_ENABLED_FOR_IAM_CONSOLE_ACCESS"}}'

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Configure authentication methods: az rest --method PATCH --uri /policies/authenticationMethodsPolicy --body '{"registrationEnforcement":{"authenticationMethodsRegistrationCampaign":{"state":"enabled"}}}'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify authentication controls including MFA and password policies

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Access rights to information and other associated assets shall be provisioned, reviewed, modified and removed in accordance with the organization's topic-specific policy on and rules for access control.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement periodic access reviews and automated removal of unused permissions.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- List unused access keys: aws iam generate-credential-report && aws iam get-credential-report --output text --query Content | base64 -d | awk -F, '$5=="true" && $6!="N/A" {print $1,$6}'
- Create unused credentials rule: aws configservice put-config-rule --config-rule '{"ConfigRuleName":"iam-user-unused-credentials-check","Source":{"Owner":"AWS","SourceIdentifier":"IAM_USER_UNUSED_CREDENTIALS_CHECK"},"InputParameters":"{\"maxCredentialUsageAge\":\"90\"}"  }'
- Use IAM Access Analyzer for unused access: aws accessanalyzer list-findings --analyzer-arn <unused-access-analyzer-arn>

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Create access review: az rest --method POST --uri /identityGovernance/accessReviews/definitions --body @quarterly-review.json

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify access rights are regularly reviewed and appropriate

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Processes and procedures shall be defined and implemented to manage the information security risks associated with the use of supplier's products or services.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement third-party access controls and monitor supplier access through dedicated roles.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Create cross-account role for supplier: aws iam create-role --role-name SupplierAccess-<vendor> --assume-role-policy-document '{"Version":"2012-10-17","Statement":[{"Effect":"Allow","Principal":{"AWS":"arn:aws:iam::<supplier-account>:root"},"Action":"sts:AssumeRole","Condition":{"StringEquals":{"sts:ExternalId":"<external-id>"}}}]}'
- Attach minimal policy: aws iam attach-role-policy --role-name SupplierAccess-<vendor> --policy-arn <scoped-policy-arn>
- Monitor supplier access via CloudTrail: aws cloudtrail lookup-events --lookup-attributes AttributeKey=Username,AttributeValue=SupplierAccess-<vendor>

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Configure B2B access: az rest --method POST --uri /policies/crossTenantAccessPolicy/partners --body '{"tenantId":"<supplier-tenant>"}'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify supplier security requirements are documented and monitored

controls.title: Addressing information security within supplier agreements

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Relevant information security requirements shall be established and agreed with each supplier based on the type of supplier relationship.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Use AWS Artifact and compliance reports to verify supplier (AWS/Azure) compliance.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Access AWS compliance reports: aws artifact get-report-metadata (use AWS Artifact console)
- Accept AWS agreements: review and accept AWS BAA, NDA via AWS Artifact Agreements
- Review shared responsibility model and document organization's responsibilities
- Store supplier agreements in S3 with versioning: aws s3 cp supplier-agreement.pdf s3://<bucket>/agreements/

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose:
- Access Azure compliance offerings via Trust Center
- Review Microsoft product terms and data protection addendum

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify supplier agreements include security requirements

controls.title: Managing information security in the ICT supply chain

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Processes and procedures shall be defined and implemented to manage the information security risks associated with the ICT products and services supply chain.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement software supply chain security with image scanning and SBOM generation.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Enable ECR image scanning: aws ecr put-image-scanning-configuration --repository-name <repo> --image-scanning-configuration scanOnPush=true
- Enable Inspector for container scanning: aws inspector2 enable --resource-types ECR
- Check scan findings: aws ecr describe-image-scan-findings --repository-name <repo> --image-id imageTag=latest
- Use CodeArtifact for dependency management: aws codeartifact create-repository --domain <domain> --repository <repo>

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Enable Defender for Containers: az security pricing create --name Containers --tier Standard

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify ICT supply chain security controls are implemented

controls.title: Monitoring, review and change management of supplier services

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The organization shall regularly monitor, review, evaluate and manage change in supplier information security practices and service delivery.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Monitor third-party service health and security posture through automated checks.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Monitor AWS service health: aws health describe-events --filter '{"eventTypeCategories":["issue","scheduledChange"]}'
- Set up health event notifications: aws events put-rule --name aws-health-alerts --event-pattern '{"source":["aws.health"]}'
- Create SNS notification: aws sns create-topic --name supplier-health-alerts && aws events put-targets --rule aws-health-alerts --targets Id=1,Arn=<sns-topic-arn>
- Review Trusted Advisor checks: aws support describe-trusted-advisor-checks --language en

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Monitor service health: az monitor activity-log alert create --name ServiceHealthAlert --condition category=ServiceHealth

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify supplier service monitoring and review processes

controls.title: Information security for use of cloud services

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Processes for acquisition, use, management and exit from cloud services shall be established in accordance with the organization's information security requirements.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement cloud governance with landing zones, guardrails, and centralized security management.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Deploy AWS Control Tower: aws controltower create-landing-zone (use console for initial setup)
- Enable Security Hub across org: aws securityhub enable-security-hub --enable-default-standards
- Create guardrails via SCPs: aws organizations create-policy --name CloudGuardrails --type SERVICE_CONTROL_POLICY --content file://guardrails.json
- Enable AWS CloudTrail org trail: aws cloudtrail create-trail --name org-trail --s3-bucket-name <bucket> --is-organization-trail --is-multi-region-trail

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose:
- Deploy Azure Landing Zone using CAF methodology
- Enable Defender for Cloud: az security pricing create --name VirtualMachines --tier Standard

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify cloud service security controls and governance

controls.title: Information security incident management planning and preparation

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The organization shall plan and prepare for managing information security incidents by defining, establishing and communicating information security incident management processes, roles and responsibilities.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Establish incident response with automated detection and notification through security services.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Enable GuardDuty: aws guardduty create-detector --enable
- Enable Security Hub: aws securityhub enable-security-hub
- Create SNS topic for incidents: aws sns create-topic --name security-incidents
- Create EventBridge rule for high-severity findings: aws events put-rule --name high-severity-findings --event-pattern '{"source":["aws.securityhub"],"detail":{"findings":{"Severity":{"Label":["CRITICAL","HIGH"]}}}}'
- Set notification target: aws events put-targets --rule high-severity-findings --targets Id=1,Arn=<sns-topic-arn>
- Store IR playbooks in SSM documents: aws ssm create-document --name IR-Playbook-Compromised-IAM --content file://playbook.json --document-type Automation

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Create Sentinel playbook: az sentinel incident create --workspace-name <ws>

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify incident response plan and automation are in place

controls.title: Assessment and decision on information security events

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The organization shall assess information security events and decide if they are to be categorized as information security incidents.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Configure automated severity classification and triage workflows for security events.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Configure Security Hub custom actions: aws securityhub create-action-target --name Triage --description 'Send finding for triage' --id triage-finding
- Set up finding aggregation: aws securityhub update-finding-aggregator --finding-aggregator-arn <arn> --region-linking-mode ALL_REGIONS
- Create custom insight for triage: aws securityhub create-insight --name UntriagedFindings --filters '{"WorkflowStatus":[{"Value":"NEW","Comparison":"EQUALS"}]}' --group-by-attribute SeverityLabel

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Configure Sentinel analytics rules: az sentinel alert-rule create --workspace-name <ws> --name SecurityEventTriage

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify security event triage and classification process

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Information security incidents shall be responded to in accordance with the documented procedures.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement automated incident response using SSM Automation and Lambda.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Create automated remediation for compromised instance: aws ssm create-document --name IR-IsolateInstance --content '{"schemaVersion":"0.3","mainSteps":[{"name":"isolateInstance","action":"aws:changeInstanceState","inputs":{"InstanceIds":["{{InstanceId}}"],"DesiredState":"stopped"}}]}' --document-type Automation
- Create auto-remediation for open security groups: aws config put-remediation-configurations --remediation-configurations file://sg-remediation.json
- Enable Detective for investigation: aws detective create-graph
- Tag incident findings: aws securityhub batch-update-findings --finding-identifiers '[{"Id":"<finding-id>","ProductArn":"<product-arn>"}]' --workflow Status=NOTIFIED

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Create Logic App playbook for auto-response: az logic workflow create --name IncidentResponse --definition @playbook.json

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify incident response procedures and automation

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Knowledge gained from information security incidents shall be used to strengthen and improve the information security controls.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Use Security Hub findings trends and CloudWatch dashboards to track and learn from incidents.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Create Security Hub insight for trends: aws securityhub create-insight --name IncidentTrends --filters '{"CreatedAt":[{"DateRange":{"Value":30,"Unit":"DAYS"}}]}' --group-by-attribute Type
- Export findings for analysis: aws securityhub get-findings --filters '{"WorkflowStatus":[{"Value":"RESOLVED","Comparison":"EQUALS"}]}'
- Create CloudWatch dashboard for metrics: aws cloudwatch put-dashboard --dashboard-name SecurityMetrics --dashboard-body file://dashboard.json
- Store post-incident reports: aws s3 cp post-incident-report.pdf s3://<bucket>/incident-reports/

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Review Sentinel incident analytics: az sentinel incident list --workspace-name <ws> --query '[?status==`Closed`]'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify post-incident review process and lessons learned

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The organization shall establish and implement procedures for the identification, collection, acquisition and preservation of evidence related to information security events.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Enable forensic evidence collection with immutable log storage and EBS snapshots.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Enable S3 Object Lock for log bucket: aws s3api put-object-lock-configuration --bucket <log-bucket> --object-lock-configuration '{"ObjectLockEnabled":"Enabled","Rule":{"DefaultRetention":{"Mode":"COMPLIANCE","Days":365}}}'
- Create forensic snapshot of EBS volume: aws ec2 create-snapshot --volume-id <vol-id> --description 'Forensic evidence - Incident <id>' --tag-specifications 'ResourceType=snapshot,Tags=[{Key=ForensicEvidence,Value=true}]'
- Enable CloudTrail log file validation: aws cloudtrail update-trail --name <trail> --enable-log-file-validation
- Copy forensic artifacts to isolated account: aws s3 sync s3://<source>/evidence/ s3://<forensic-bucket>/case-<id>/

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Create immutable blob storage: az storage container-rm create --storage-account <acct> --name evidence && az storage container immutability-policy create --account-name <acct> -c evidence --period 365

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify evidence collection and preservation procedures

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The organization shall plan how to maintain information security at an appropriate level during disruption.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Ensure security services are deployed multi-region and DR plans include security controls.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Enable multi-region CloudTrail: aws cloudtrail update-trail --name <trail> --is-multi-region-trail
- Enable GuardDuty in all regions: for region in $(aws ec2 describe-regions --query 'Regions[].RegionName' --output text); do aws guardduty create-detector --enable --region $region; done
- Replicate security configs to DR region: aws s3api put-bucket-replication --bucket <config-bucket> --replication-configuration file://replication.json
- Test failover: aws rds failover-db-cluster --db-cluster-identifier <cluster>

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Configure Azure Site Recovery: az backup protection enable-for-vm --resource-group <rg> --vault-name <vault> --vm <vm> --policy-name DefaultPolicy

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify security controls remain active during business continuity events

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: ICT readiness shall be planned, implemented, maintained and tested based on business continuity objectives.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement and test automated backups using AWS Backup with cross-region replication.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Create backup plan: aws backup create-backup-plan --backup-plan '{"BackupPlanName":"DailyBackup","Rules":[{"RuleName":"DailyRule","TargetBackupVaultName":"Default","ScheduleExpression":"cron(0 1 * * ? *)","Lifecycle":{"DeleteAfterDays":90}}]}'
- Assign resources to plan: aws backup create-backup-selection --backup-plan-id <plan-id> --backup-selection '{"SelectionName":"AllTagged","IamRoleArn":"<role-arn>","ListOfTags":[{"ConditionType":"STRINGEQUALS","ConditionKey":"Backup","ConditionValue":"true"}]}'
- Enable cross-region copy: add CopyActions to backup rule
- Test restore: aws backup start-restore-job --recovery-point-arn <arn> --iam-role-arn <role-arn> --metadata file://restore-metadata.json

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Create backup policy: az backup policy create --resource-group <rg> --vault-name <vault> --name DailyPolicy --policy @backup-policy.json

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify ICT systems have tested backup and recovery capabilities

controls.title: Legal, statutory, regulatory and contractual requirements

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Legal, statutory, regulatory and contractual requirements relevant to information security and the organization's approach to meet these requirements shall be identified, documented and kept up to date.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Use Security Hub compliance standards and AWS Audit Manager to track regulatory compliance.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Enable compliance standards in Security Hub: aws securityhub batch-enable-standards --standards-subscription-requests StandardsArn=arn:aws:securityhub:::standards/cis-aws-foundations-benchmark/v/1.4.0
- Create Audit Manager assessment: aws auditmanager create-assessment --name ISO27001Assessment --framework-id <framework-id> --assessment-reports-destination '{"destinationType":"S3","destination":"s3://<bucket>/audit-reports"}' --roles '[{"roleArn":"<arn>","roleType":"PROCESS_OWNER"}]' --scope '{"awsAccounts":[{"id":"<account-id>"}]}'
- List available frameworks: aws auditmanager list-assessment-frameworks --framework-type Standard

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - View compliance in Defender for Cloud: az security regulatory-compliance-standards list

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify compliance requirements are documented and tracked

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The organization shall implement appropriate procedures to protect intellectual property rights.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement DLP controls and license tracking to protect intellectual property.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Enable Macie for sensitive data discovery: aws macie2 enable-macie
- Create custom data identifiers for proprietary patterns: aws macie2 create-custom-data-identifier --name ProprietaryCode --regex '(?i)(proprietary|confidential|trade.?secret)' --keywords '["internal only"]'
- Restrict code repository access: configure CodeCommit or CodeArtifact repository policies
- Use License Manager: aws license-manager create-license-configuration --name 'Software Licenses' --license-counting-type Instance

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Enable Microsoft Purview DLP: configure DLP policies via Microsoft 365 compliance center

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify intellectual property protection controls

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Records shall be protected from loss, destruction, falsification, unauthorized access and unauthorized release.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Enable S3 versioning, encryption, Object Lock and access logging for record storage.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Enable versioning: aws s3api put-bucket-versioning --bucket <records-bucket> --versioning-configuration Status=Enabled
- Enable default encryption: aws s3api put-bucket-encryption --bucket <records-bucket> --server-side-encryption-configuration '{"Rules":[{"ApplyServerSideEncryptionByDefault":{"SSEAlgorithm":"aws:kms","KMSMasterKeyID":"<key-id>"}}]}'
- Enable Object Lock: aws s3api put-object-lock-configuration --bucket <records-bucket> --object-lock-configuration '{"ObjectLockEnabled":"Enabled","Rule":{"DefaultRetention":{"Mode":"GOVERNANCE","Days":2555}}}'
- Enable access logging: aws s3api put-bucket-logging --bucket <records-bucket> --bucket-logging-status '{"LoggingEnabled":{"TargetBucket":"<log-bucket>","TargetPrefix":"records-access/"}}'

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Enable soft delete and versioning: az storage blob service-properties update --account-name <acct> --enable-versioning true --delete-retention-days 365

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify records are protected with versioning, encryption, and access controls

controls.title: Privacy and protection of personal identifiable information (PII)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The organization shall identify and meet the requirements regarding the preservation of privacy and protection of PII as required in applicable legislation and regulation and contractual requirements.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Use Macie to discover and classify PII, and implement data protection controls.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Enable Macie: aws macie2 enable-macie
- Create PII discovery job: aws macie2 create-classification-job --job-type ONE_TIME --s3-job-definition '{"bucketDefinitions":[{"accountId":"<acct>","buckets":["<bucket>"]}]}' --managed-data-identifier-selector ALL --name PIIDiscovery
- Review PII findings: aws macie2 get-finding-statistics --group-by type
- Block public access for PII buckets: aws s3api put-public-access-block --bucket <bucket> --public-access-block-configuration BlockPublicAcls=true,IgnorePublicAcls=true,BlockPublicPolicy=true,RestrictPublicBuckets=true

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Enable Purview scanning: az purview account create --resource-group <rg> --name <acct>

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify PII is discovered, classified, and protected

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The organization's approach to managing information security and its implementation including people, processes and technologies shall be reviewed independently at planned intervals, or when significant changes occur.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Use AWS Audit Manager for automated evidence collection and schedule independent audits.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Create Audit Manager assessment: aws auditmanager create-assessment --name IndependentReview --framework-id <iso-27001-framework-id> --assessment-reports-destination '{"destinationType":"S3","destination":"s3://<bucket>/audit"}' --roles '[{"roleArn":"<arn>","roleType":"PROCESS_OWNER"}]' --scope '{"awsAccounts":[{"id":"<account-id>"}]}'
- Generate assessment report: aws auditmanager create-assessment-report --name AnnualReview --assessment-id <id>
- Enable Security Hub CIS benchmark: aws securityhub batch-enable-standards --standards-subscription-requests StandardsArn=arn:aws:securityhub:::standards/cis-aws-foundations-benchmark/v/1.4.0

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - View compliance score: az security regulatory-compliance-standards list

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify independent security reviews are conducted

controls.title: Compliance with policies, rules and standards for information security

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Compliance with the organization's established information security policy, topic-specific policies, rules and standards shall be regularly reviewed.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Use AWS Config conformance packs and Security Hub for continuous compliance monitoring.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Deploy conformance pack: aws configservice put-conformance-pack --conformance-pack-name Operational-Best-Practices-for-ISO-27001 --template-s3-uri s3://aws-configservice-templates/Operational-Best-Practices-for-ISO27001-2013.template.yaml
- Check compliance: aws configservice describe-conformance-pack-compliance --conformance-pack-name Operational-Best-Practices-for-ISO-27001
- Create compliance dashboard: aws cloudwatch put-dashboard --dashboard-name ComplianceStatus --dashboard-body file://compliance-dashboard.json

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Assign ISO 27001 policy initiative: az policy assignment create --name ISO27001 --policy-set-definition '/providers/Microsoft.Authorization/policySetDefinitions/89c6cddc-1c73-4ac1-b19c-54d1a15a42f2'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify compliance monitoring and reporting is active

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Operating procedures for information processing facilities shall be documented and made available to personnel who need them.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Document operational runbooks in SSM and make them accessible to operations teams.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Create SSM runbook: aws ssm create-document --name OpsRunbook-PatchManagement --content file://patch-runbook.json --document-type Automation --document-format JSON
- Share document with team: aws ssm modify-document-permission --name OpsRunbook-PatchManagement --permission-type Share --account-ids-to-add <account-id>
- List available documents: aws ssm list-documents --filters Key=DocumentType,Values=Automation
- Store procedures in S3 wiki: aws s3 sync ./runbooks/ s3://<bucket>/runbooks/

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Create Azure Automation runbook: az automation runbook create --resource-group <rg> --automation-account-name <acct> --name PatchManagement --type PowerShell

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify operating procedures are documented and accessible

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Background verification checks on all candidates to become personnel shall be carried out prior to joining the organization and on an ongoing basis taking into account applicable laws, regulations and ethics and be proportional to the business requirements, the classification of the information to be accessed and the perceived risks.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement identity verification and access provisioning controls that require screening completion before granting access.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Create IAM user only after screening flag in SSM: aws ssm get-parameter --name /hr/screening/<user>/status --query 'Parameter.Value'
- Tag users with screening status: aws iam tag-user --user-name <user> --tags Key=ScreeningCompleted,Value=true Key=ScreeningDate,Value=2026-01-15
- Create Config rule to check user tags: aws configservice put-config-rule --config-rule file://screening-tag-rule.json
- List users missing screening tag: aws configservice get-compliance-details-by-config-rule --config-rule-name RequireScreeningTag --compliance-types NON_COMPLIANT

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Check user provisioning status: az ad user show --id <user-id> --query '{displayName:displayName, accountEnabled:accountEnabled}'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify background screening processes are documented and automated where possible

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The employment contractual agreements shall state the personnel's and the organization's responsibilities for information security.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Store signed employment agreements with security clauses and track acknowledgment digitally.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Store signed agreements in S3: aws s3 cp employment-agreement-signed.pdf s3://<hr-bucket>/agreements/<user>/
- Enable versioning on HR bucket: aws s3api put-bucket-versioning --bucket <hr-bucket> --versioning-configuration Status=Enabled
- Track acknowledgment in SSM Parameter Store: aws ssm put-parameter --name /hr/agreements/<user>/security-acknowledged --value 'true' --type String
- Enforce encryption on HR bucket: aws s3api put-bucket-encryption --bucket <hr-bucket> --server-side-encryption-configuration '{"Rules":[{"ApplyServerSideEncryptionByDefault":{"SSEAlgorithm":"aws:kms"}}]}'

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Upload agreement to secure blob: az storage blob upload --container-name hr-agreements --file agreement.pdf --name <user>/agreement.pdf --account-name <storage>

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify employment agreements include security responsibilities and are acknowledged

controls.title: Information security awareness, education and training

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Personnel of the organization and relevant interested parties shall receive appropriate information security awareness, education and training and regular updates of the organization's information security policy, topic-specific policies and procedures, as relevant for their job function.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Track security training completion and enforce training requirements before granting elevated access.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Store training records in SSM: aws ssm put-parameter --name /training/<user>/security-awareness --value '{"completed":true,"date":"2026-01-15","score":95}' --type String
- Tag IAM users with training status: aws iam tag-user --user-name <user> --tags Key=SecurityTrainingCompleted,Value=2026-01-15
- Create Config rule for training tags: aws configservice put-config-rule --config-rule file://training-tag-required.json
- Query non-compliant users: aws configservice get-compliance-details-by-config-rule --config-rule-name RequireTrainingTag --compliance-types NON_COMPLIANT

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Check training compliance via Graph API: az rest --method GET --uri /users/<user-id>/memberOf

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify security awareness training is delivered and tracked

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A disciplinary process shall be formalized and communicated to take actions against personnel and other relevant interested parties who have committed an information security policy violation.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Document disciplinary procedures and implement automated detection of policy violations.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Store disciplinary policy in S3: aws s3 cp disciplinary-policy.pdf s3://<policy-bucket>/hr-policies/
- Enable CloudTrail for user activity monitoring: aws cloudtrail create-trail --name UserActivityTrail --s3-bucket-name <trail-bucket> --is-multi-region-trail
- Create CloudWatch alarm for unauthorized access attempts: aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm --alarm-name UnauthorizedAccessAttempts --metric-name UnauthorizedAttempts --namespace Security --threshold 5 --comparison-operator GreaterThanThreshold --evaluation-periods 1 --period 300 --statistic Sum --alarm-actions <sns-arn>
- Create EventBridge rule for policy violations: aws events put-rule --name PolicyViolationDetection --event-pattern '{"source":["aws.guardduty"],"detail-type":["GuardDuty Finding"]}'

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Store policy in SharePoint: az storage blob upload --container-name policies --file disciplinary-policy.pdf --name hr-policies/disciplinary-policy.pdf

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify disciplinary process is documented and security violations are tracked

controls.title: Responsibilities after employment termination or change

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Information security responsibilities and duties that remain valid after termination or change of employment shall be defined, enforced and communicated to relevant personnel and other interested parties.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Automate access revocation upon employment termination and maintain audit trail of offboarding actions.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- List user access keys for revocation: aws iam list-access-keys --user-name <user>
- Deactivate access keys: aws iam update-access-key --user-name <user> --access-key-id <key-id> --status Inactive
- Remove console access: aws iam delete-login-profile --user-name <user>
- Deactivate MFA devices: aws iam deactivate-mfa-device --user-name <user> --serial-number <mfa-arn>
- Remove from all groups: aws iam list-groups-for-user --user-name <user> --query 'Groups[].GroupName' --output text | xargs -I {} aws iam remove-user-from-group --user-name <user> --group-name {}
- Tag user as terminated: aws iam tag-user --user-name <user> --tags Key=Status,Value=Terminated Key=TerminationDate,Value=2026-01-15

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose:
- Disable user account: az ad user update --id <user-id> --account-enabled false
- Revoke all sessions: az rest --method POST --uri /users/<user-id>/revokeSignInSessions

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify offboarding procedures revoke access and enforce post-employment obligations

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Confidentiality or non-disclosure agreements reflecting the organization's needs for the protection of information shall be identified, documented, regularly reviewed and signed by personnel and other relevant interested parties.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Store signed NDAs in encrypted storage with versioning and track review dates.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Store signed NDAs in S3: aws s3 cp nda-signed.pdf s3://<hr-bucket>/ndas/<user>/
- Enable encryption on HR bucket: aws s3api put-bucket-encryption --bucket <hr-bucket> --server-side-encryption-configuration '{"Rules":[{"ApplyServerSideEncryptionByDefault":{"SSEAlgorithm":"aws:kms","KMSMasterKeyID":"<kms-key-id>"}}]}'
- Track NDA status in SSM: aws ssm put-parameter --name /hr/nda/<user>/status --value '{"signed":true,"date":"2026-01-15","review_due":"2027-01-15"}' --type String
- Block public access on HR bucket: aws s3api put-public-access-block --bucket <hr-bucket> --public-access-block-configuration BlockPublicAcls=true,IgnorePublicAcls=true,BlockPublicPolicy=true,RestrictPublicBuckets=true

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Upload NDA to secure storage: az storage blob upload --container-name ndas --file nda-signed.pdf --name <user>/nda-signed.pdf --account-name <storage>

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify NDAs are signed and stored securely with review tracking

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Security measures shall be implemented when personnel are working remotely to protect information accessed, processed or stored outside the organization's premises.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement VPN access controls, endpoint compliance checks, and secure remote access policies.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Create Client VPN endpoint: aws ec2 create-client-vpn-endpoint --client-cidr-block 10.0.0.0/16 --server-certificate-arn <cert-arn> --authentication-options Type=certificate-authentication,MutualAuthentication={ClientRootCertificateChainArn=<client-cert-arn>} --connection-log-options Enabled=true,CloudwatchLogGroup=/aws/vpn/connections
- Associate VPN with subnet: aws ec2 associate-client-vpn-target-network --client-vpn-endpoint-id <vpn-id> --subnet-id <subnet-id>
- Add authorization rule: aws ec2 authorize-client-vpn-ingress --client-vpn-endpoint-id <vpn-id> --target-network-cidr 10.0.0.0/16 --authorize-all-groups
- Verify VPN connections: aws ec2 describe-client-vpn-connections --client-vpn-endpoint-id <vpn-id>

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Create VPN gateway: az network vnet-gateway create --name RemoteVPN --resource-group <rg> --vnet <vnet> --gateway-type Vpn --vpn-type RouteBased --sku VpnGw1

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify remote working security controls including VPN and endpoint protection

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The organization shall provide a mechanism for personnel to report observed or suspected information security events through appropriate channels in a timely manner.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement automated security event detection and reporting channels for personnel.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Enable GuardDuty for automated event detection: aws guardduty create-detector --enable --finding-publishing-frequency FIFTEEN_MINUTES
- Create SNS topic for security event reporting: aws sns create-topic --name SecurityEventReporting
- Subscribe security team: aws sns subscribe --topic-arn <topic-arn> --protocol email --notification-endpoint security-team@example.com
- Create EventBridge rule to route security events: aws events put-rule --name SecurityEventNotification --event-pattern '{"source":["aws.guardduty","aws.securityhub"],"detail-type":["GuardDuty Finding","Security Hub Findings - Imported"]}'
- Add SNS target to EventBridge rule: aws events put-targets --rule SecurityEventNotification --targets Id=1,Arn=<topic-arn>

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Configure Defender for Cloud alerts: az security alert list --query '[].{Name:alertDisplayName, Severity:severity}'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify security event reporting mechanisms are in place and accessible

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Security perimeters shall be defined and used to protect areas that contain information and other associated assets.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Restrict workloads to approved AWS regions and document physical security perimeter requirements.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Create SCP to restrict regions: aws organizations create-policy --name AllowedRegionsOnly --type SERVICE_CONTROL_POLICY --content '{"Version":"2012-10-17","Statement":[{"Effect":"Deny","Action":"*","Resource":"*","Condition":{"StringNotEquals":{"aws:RequestedRegion":["us-east-1","eu-west-1"]}}}]}'
- Attach SCP to organizational unit: aws organizations attach-policy --policy-id <policy-id> --target-id <ou-id>
- Verify region restrictions: aws ec2 describe-regions --query 'Regions[].RegionName'
- Store physical security documentation in S3: aws s3 cp physical-security-perimeter-doc.pdf s3://<policy-bucket>/physical-security/

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Create allowed locations policy: az policy assignment create --name AllowedLocations --policy '/providers/Microsoft.Authorization/policyDefinitions/e56962a6-4747-49cd-b67b-bf8b01975c4c' --params '{"listOfAllowedLocations":{"value":["eastus","westeurope"]}}'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify physical security perimeters are defined for cloud infrastructure regions

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Secure areas shall be protected by appropriate entry controls and access points.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Monitor console and API access patterns to detect unauthorized physical or logical entry.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Enable CloudTrail for management events: aws cloudtrail create-trail --name ManagementAuditTrail --s3-bucket-name <trail-bucket> --is-multi-region-trail --enable-log-file-validation
- Start logging: aws cloudtrail start-logging --name ManagementAuditTrail
- Create metric filter for console sign-ins: aws logs put-metric-filter --log-group-name CloudTrail/ManagementEvents --filter-name ConsoleSignIns --filter-pattern '{($.eventName=ConsoleLogin)}' --metric-transformations metricName=ConsoleSignInCount,metricNamespace=Security,metricValue=1
- Create alarm for unusual sign-ins: aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm --alarm-name UnusualConsoleSignIn --metric-name ConsoleSignInCount --namespace Security --threshold 10 --comparison-operator GreaterThanThreshold --evaluation-periods 1 --period 3600 --statistic Sum --alarm-actions <sns-arn>

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Review sign-in logs: az monitor activity-log list --query "[?contains(operationName.value,'Microsoft.Authorization')]" --start-time 2026-01-01

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify physical entry controls via CloudTrail and console access monitoring

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Physical security for offices, rooms and facilities shall be designed and implemented.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Document physical facility security controls and enforce network segmentation for sensitive zones.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Store facility security plans in S3: aws s3 cp facility-security-plan.pdf s3://<policy-bucket>/physical-security/
- Create VPC for sensitive workloads: aws ec2 create-vpc --cidr-block 10.1.0.0/16 --tag-specifications 'ResourceType=vpc,Tags=[{Key=Zone,Value=Restricted}]'
- Create private subnet: aws ec2 create-subnet --vpc-id <vpc-id> --cidr-block 10.1.1.0/24 --tag-specifications 'ResourceType=subnet,Tags=[{Key=Zone,Value=Restricted}]'
- Verify no public subnets in restricted VPC: aws ec2 describe-subnets --filters Name=vpc-id,Values=<vpc-id> --query 'Subnets[?MapPublicIpOnLaunch==`true`]'

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Create network security group for restricted zone: az network nsg create --resource-group <rg> --name RestrictedZoneNSG

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify facility security documentation and restricted area configurations

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Premises shall be continuously monitored for unauthorized physical access.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement continuous monitoring of infrastructure access using CloudTrail, GuardDuty, and Security Hub.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Enable GuardDuty: aws guardduty create-detector --enable --finding-publishing-frequency FIFTEEN_MINUTES
- Enable Security Hub: aws securityhub enable-security-hub --enable-default-standards
- Create CloudWatch dashboard for security monitoring: aws cloudwatch put-dashboard --dashboard-name SecurityMonitoring --dashboard-body file://security-dashboard.json
- Enable VPC Flow Logs: aws ec2 create-flow-logs --resource-type VPC --resource-ids <vpc-id> --traffic-type ALL --log-destination-type cloud-watch-logs --log-group-name /aws/vpc/flowlogs

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Enable Defender for Cloud: az security pricing create --name VirtualMachines --tier Standard

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify continuous monitoring of access patterns and anomaly detection

controls.title: Protecting against physical and environmental threats

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Protection against physical and environmental threats, such as natural disasters and other intentional or unintentional physical threats to infrastructure shall be designed and implemented.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Deploy workloads across multiple availability zones and configure disaster recovery.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Verify RDS multi-AZ deployment: aws rds describe-db-instances --query 'DBInstances[].{DBInstanceIdentifier:DBInstanceIdentifier,MultiAZ:MultiAZ,AvailabilityZone:AvailabilityZone}'
- Enable S3 cross-region replication: aws s3api put-bucket-replication --bucket <source-bucket> --replication-configuration file://replication-config.json
- Describe EC2 instances across AZs: aws ec2 describe-instances --query 'Reservations[].Instances[].{InstanceId:InstanceId,AZ:Placement.AvailabilityZone}'
- Create backup plan: aws backup create-backup-plan --backup-plan '{"BackupPlanName":"DR-Plan","Rules":[{"RuleName":"DailyBackup","TargetBackupVaultName":"Default","ScheduleExpression":"cron(0 12 * * ? *)","Lifecycle":{"DeleteAfterDays":90}}]}'

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Check availability zones: az vm list --query '[].{Name:name, Zones:zones}' -o table

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify multi-AZ and disaster recovery configurations for environmental resilience

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Security measures for working in secure areas shall be designed and implemented.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement strict access controls for sensitive environments using IAM policies and network isolation.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Create IAM policy restricting access to production: aws iam create-policy --policy-name RestrictedProductionAccess --policy-document '{"Version":"2012-10-17","Statement":[{"Effect":"Allow","Action":["ec2:Describe*","rds:Describe*"],"Resource":"*","Condition":{"StringEquals":{"aws:RequestedRegion":"us-east-1"}}}]}'
- Create restricted IAM group: aws iam create-group --group-name SecureAreaAccess
- Attach policy to group: aws iam attach-group-policy --group-name SecureAreaAccess --policy-arn <policy-arn>
- Enable session logging with SSM: aws ssm start-session --target <instance-id> --document-name AWS-StartInteractiveCommand

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Create PIM eligible assignment: az rest --method POST --uri /providers/Microsoft.Authorization/roleEligibilityScheduleRequests --body @pim-request.json

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify access controls for secure/restricted environments

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Clear desk rules for papers and removable storage media and clear screen rules for information processing facilities shall be defined and appropriately enforced.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Enforce session timeouts and screen lock policies for cloud console and CLI access.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Set IAM password policy with session controls: aws iam update-account-password-policy --max-password-age 90 --password-reuse-prevention 24 --minimum-password-length 14 --require-symbols --require-numbers --require-uppercase-characters --require-lowercase-characters
- Set maximum CLI session duration for roles: aws iam update-role --role-name <role> --max-session-duration 3600
- Create SCP to enforce session duration: aws organizations create-policy --name EnforceSessionDuration --type SERVICE_CONTROL_POLICY --content file://session-duration-scp.json
- Check current session durations: aws iam list-roles --query 'Roles[].{RoleName:RoleName,MaxSessionDuration:MaxSessionDuration}'

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Set conditional access session controls: az rest --method POST --uri /identity/conditionalAccess/policies --body @session-policy.json

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify screen lock policies and session timeout configurations

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Equipment shall be sited securely and protected.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Ensure infrastructure is deployed in approved regions with appropriate protections.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- List all resources by region: aws ec2 describe-instances --query 'Reservations[].Instances[].{InstanceId:InstanceId,Region:Placement.AvailabilityZone}' --region <region>
- Verify dedicated hosts if required: aws ec2 describe-hosts --query 'Hosts[].{HostId:HostId,State:State,InstanceType:HostProperties.InstanceType}'
- Check placement groups: aws ec2 describe-placement-groups --query 'PlacementGroups[].{Name:GroupName,Strategy:Strategy,State:State}'
- Verify Nitro-based instances: aws ec2 describe-instance-types --filters Name=hypervisor,Values=nitro --query 'InstanceTypes[].InstanceType'

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - List resource locations: az resource list --query '[].{Name:name, Location:location}' -o table

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify infrastructure deployment in compliant and protected locations

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Off-site assets shall be protected.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Ensure all off-premises data is encrypted in transit and at rest with appropriate key management.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Verify S3 bucket encryption: aws s3api get-bucket-encryption --bucket <bucket>
- Enable default encryption on all buckets: aws s3api put-bucket-encryption --bucket <bucket> --server-side-encryption-configuration '{"Rules":[{"ApplyServerSideEncryptionByDefault":{"SSEAlgorithm":"aws:kms"}}]}'
- Check EBS encryption by default: aws ec2 get-ebs-encryption-by-default
- Enable EBS encryption by default: aws ec2 enable-ebs-encryption-by-default
- Verify SSL/TLS on load balancers: aws elbv2 describe-listeners --load-balancer-arn <lb-arn> --query 'Listeners[].{Protocol:Protocol,SslPolicy:SslPolicy}'

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Check storage account encryption: az storage account show --name <account> --query encryption

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify encryption and protection of data stored or transferred off-premises

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Storage media shall be managed through their life cycle of acquisition, use, transportation and disposal in accordance with the organization's classification scheme and handling requirements.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement lifecycle policies for storage media and ensure encryption throughout the lifecycle.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Create S3 lifecycle policy: aws s3api put-bucket-lifecycle-configuration --bucket <bucket> --lifecycle-configuration '{"Rules":[{"ID":"MediaRetention","Status":"Enabled","Transitions":[{"Days":90,"StorageClass":"GLACIER"}],"Expiration":{"Days":365},"Filter":{"Prefix":""}}]}'
- Verify EBS volume encryption: aws ec2 describe-volumes --query 'Volumes[?Encrypted==`false`].{VolumeId:VolumeId,Size:Size,State:State}'
- Check KMS key rotation: aws kms get-key-rotation-status --key-id <key-id>
- Enable automatic key rotation: aws kms enable-key-rotation --key-id <key-id>

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Set blob lifecycle management: az storage account management-policy create --account-name <account> --resource-group <rg> --policy @lifecycle-policy.json

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify storage media lifecycle management and encryption

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Information processing facilities shall be protected from power failures and other disruptions caused by failures in supporting utilities.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Configure multi-AZ deployments and auto-scaling to protect against infrastructure failures.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Verify multi-AZ RDS: aws rds describe-db-instances --query 'DBInstances[].{DB:DBInstanceIdentifier,MultiAZ:MultiAZ}'
- Check Auto Scaling groups: aws autoscaling describe-auto-scaling-groups --query 'AutoScalingGroups[].{Name:AutoScalingGroupName,MinSize:MinSize,MaxSize:MaxSize,AZs:AvailabilityZones}'
- Verify ELB cross-zone load balancing: aws elbv2 describe-load-balancers --query 'LoadBalancers[].{Name:LoadBalancerName,AZs:AvailabilityZones[].ZoneName}'
- Check Route 53 health checks: aws route53 list-health-checks --query 'HealthChecks[].{Id:Id,Type:HealthCheckConfig.Type,FQDN:HealthCheckConfig.FullyQualifiedDomainName}'

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Check availability sets: az vm availability-set list --query '[].{Name:name, FaultDomains:platformFaultDomainCount}'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify redundancy and failover configurations for supporting infrastructure

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Cables carrying power, data or supporting information services shall be protected from interception, interference or damage.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Ensure all network communications use encrypted channels and private connectivity where available.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Verify VPN connections: aws ec2 describe-vpn-connections --query 'VpnConnections[].{VpnId:VpnConnectionId,State:State,Type:Type}'
- Check Direct Connect connections: aws directconnect describe-connections --query 'connections[].{ConnectionId:connectionId,State:connectionState,Bandwidth:bandwidth}'
- Verify TLS on load balancers: aws elbv2 describe-listeners --load-balancer-arn <lb-arn> --query 'Listeners[].{Protocol:Protocol,SslPolicy:SslPolicy}'
- Check VPC endpoints for private connectivity: aws ec2 describe-vpc-endpoints --query 'VpcEndpoints[].{Service:ServiceName,State:State,Type:VpcEndpointType}'

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Check ExpressRoute circuits: az network express-route list --query '[].{Name:name, ServiceProviderProperties:serviceProviderProperties}'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify network encryption and secure connectivity configurations

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Equipment shall be maintained correctly to ensure availability, integrity and confidentiality of information.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement automated patching using Systems Manager and track maintenance windows.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Create SSM maintenance window: aws ssm create-maintenance-window --name WeeklyPatching --schedule 'cron(0 2 ? * SUN *)' --duration 4 --cutoff 1 --allow-unassociated-targets
- Register targets: aws ssm register-target-with-maintenance-window --window-id <window-id> --resource-type INSTANCE --targets Key=tag:PatchGroup,Values=Production
- Create patch baseline: aws ssm create-patch-baseline --name ProductionBaseline --operating-system AMAZON_LINUX_2 --approval-rules '{"PatchRules":[{"PatchFilterGroup":{"PatchFilters":[{"Key":"SEVERITY","Values":["Critical","Important"]}]},"ApproveAfterDays":7}]}'
- Check patch compliance: aws ssm describe-instance-patch-states --instance-ids <instance-id>

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Enable Update Management: az maintenance configuration create --resource-group <rg> --name WeeklyPatching --maintenance-scope InGuestPatch --install-patches-linux-parameters '{"classificationsToInclude":["Critical","Security"]}'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify patching and maintenance schedules for managed infrastructure

controls.title: Secure disposal or re-use of equipment

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Items of equipment containing storage media shall be verified to ensure that any sensitive data and licensed software has been removed or securely overwritten prior to disposal or re-use.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Ensure EBS volumes and snapshots are securely deleted and S3 objects have lifecycle expiration.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- List unattached EBS volumes: aws ec2 describe-volumes --filters Name=status,Values=available --query 'Volumes[].{VolumeId:VolumeId,Size:Size,Encrypted:Encrypted,CreateTime:CreateTime}'
- Delete unattached volume: aws ec2 delete-volume --volume-id <vol-id>
- List old snapshots: aws ec2 describe-snapshots --owner-ids self --query 'Snapshots[?StartTime<`2025-01-01`].{SnapshotId:SnapshotId,StartTime:StartTime}'
- Delete old snapshot: aws ec2 delete-snapshot --snapshot-id <snap-id>
- Verify S3 object deletion with MFA: aws s3api put-bucket-versioning --bucket <bucket> --versioning-configuration Status=Enabled,MFADelete=Enabled --mfa '<mfa-serial> <mfa-code>'

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose:
- List unattached disks: az disk list --query '[?diskState==`Unattached`].{Name:name, DiskSizeGb:diskSizeGb}'
- Delete unattached disk: az disk delete --name <disk-name> --resource-group <rg> --yes

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify secure data deletion and volume cleanup procedures

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Information stored on, processed by or accessible via user endpoint devices shall be protected.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement endpoint device management and conditional access policies.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Create IAM policy requiring MFA: aws iam create-policy --policy-name RequireMFA --policy-document '{"Version":"2012-10-17","Statement":[{"Effect":"Deny","NotAction":["iam:CreateVirtualMFADevice","iam:EnableMFADevice","iam:ListMFADevices"],"Resource":"*","Condition":{"BoolIfExists":{"aws:MultiFactorAuthPresent":"false"}}}]}'
- Verify MFA is enabled for all users: aws iam generate-credential-report && aws iam get-credential-report --query 'Content' --output text | base64 -d | cut -d',' -f1,4,8
- Create SSM association for endpoint compliance: aws ssm create-association --name AWS-GatherSoftwareInventory --targets Key=instanceids,Values=* --schedule-expression 'rate(12 hours)'
- Check SSM managed instances: aws ssm describe-instance-information --query 'InstanceInformationList[].{InstanceId:InstanceId,PingStatus:PingStatus,PlatformType:PlatformType}'

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - List Intune managed devices: az rest --method GET --uri /deviceManagement/managedDevices

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify endpoint device security policies and access controls

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The allocation and use of privileged access rights shall be restricted and managed.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Restrict administrative access using least privilege, require MFA for privileged actions, and monitor usage.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- List users with AdministratorAccess: aws iam list_entities_for_policy --policy-arn arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AdministratorAccess --entity-filter User
- Check for root account usage: aws iam generate-credential-report && aws iam get-credential-report --query 'Content' --output text | base64 -d | head -2
- Create Config rule for root usage: aws configservice put-config-rule --config-rule '{"ConfigRuleName":"root-account-mfa-enabled","Source":{"Owner":"AWS","SourceIdentifier":"ROOT_ACCOUNT_MFA_ENABLED"}}'
- Enable IAM Access Analyzer: aws accessanalyzer create-analyzer --analyzer-name PrivilegeAnalyzer --type ACCOUNT
- List Access Analyzer findings: aws accessanalyzer list-findings --analyzer-arn <analyzer-arn> --filter '{"resourceType":{"eq":["AWS::IAM::Role"]}}'

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - List Global Administrators: az role assignment list --role 'Global Administrator' --query '[].{Principal:principalName}'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify privileged access is restricted and monitored

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Access to information and other associated assets shall be restricted in accordance with the established topic-specific policy on access control.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement resource-based access controls and least privilege policies.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Review S3 bucket policies: aws s3api get-bucket-policy --bucket <bucket>
- Check for public S3 buckets: aws s3api list-buckets --query 'Buckets[].Name' | xargs -I {} aws s3api get-public-access-block --bucket {}
- Enable S3 Block Public Access at account level: aws s3control put-public-access-block --account-id <account-id> --public-access-block-configuration BlockPublicAcls=true,IgnorePublicAcls=true,BlockPublicPolicy=true,RestrictPublicBuckets=true
- Review KMS key policies: aws kms get-key-policy --key-id <key-id> --policy-name default
- Create Config rule for public access: aws configservice put-config-rule --config-rule '{"ConfigRuleName":"s3-bucket-public-read-prohibited","Source":{"Owner":"AWS","SourceIdentifier":"S3_BUCKET_PUBLIC_READ_PROHIBITED"}}'

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Check storage account network rules: az storage account show --name <account> --query networkRuleSet

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify information access restrictions are enforced through IAM and resource policies

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Read and write access to source code, development tools and software libraries shall be appropriately managed.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Restrict source code repository access and implement branch protection.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- List CodeCommit repositories: aws codecommit list-repositories
- Get repository permissions: aws codecommit get-repository --repository-name <repo>
- Create IAM policy restricting repo access: aws iam create-policy --policy-name CodeCommitReadOnly --policy-document '{"Version":"2012-10-17","Statement":[{"Effect":"Allow","Action":["codecommit:GitPull","codecommit:Get*","codecommit:List*"],"Resource":"arn:aws:codecommit:<region>:<account>:<repo>"}]}'
- Set approval rule template: aws codecommit create-approval-rule-template --approval-rule-template-name RequireTwoApprovals --approval-rule-template-content '{"Version":"2018-11-08","DestinationReferences":["refs/heads/main"],"Statements":[{"Type":"Approvers","NumberOfApprovalsNeeded":2}]}'

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - List DevOps repositories: az repos list --query '[].{Name:name, DefaultBranch:defaultBranch}'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify source code access controls and repository security

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Secure authentication technologies and procedures shall be established and implemented based on information access restrictions and the topic-specific policy on access control.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Enforce strong authentication with MFA, password complexity, and federation where possible.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Set strong password policy: aws iam update-account-password-policy --minimum-password-length 14 --require-symbols --require-numbers --require-uppercase-characters --require-lowercase-characters --max-password-age 90 --password-reuse-prevention 24 --hard-expiry
