- List users without MFA: aws iam generate-credential-report && aws iam get-credential-report --query 'Content' --output text | base64 -d | awk -F',' '$4=="true" && $8=="false" {print $1}'
- Create SAML identity provider: aws iam create-saml-provider --saml-metadata-document file://metadata.xml --name CorporateIdP
- Verify MFA on root: aws iam get-account-summary --query 'SummaryMap.AccountMFAEnabled'

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Enforce MFA via conditional access: az rest --method POST --uri /identity/conditionalAccess/policies --body @mfa-policy.json

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify secure authentication mechanisms including MFA and password policies

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The use of resources shall be monitored and adjusted in line with current and expected capacity requirements.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement capacity monitoring with CloudWatch and auto-scaling policies.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Check Auto Scaling configurations: aws autoscaling describe-auto-scaling-groups --query 'AutoScalingGroups[].{Name:AutoScalingGroupName,Min:MinSize,Max:MaxSize,Desired:DesiredCapacity}'
- Create CPU utilization alarm: aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm --alarm-name HighCPU --metric-name CPUUtilization --namespace AWS/EC2 --threshold 80 --comparison-operator GreaterThanThreshold --evaluation-periods 3 --period 300 --statistic Average --alarm-actions <sns-arn>
- Enable Compute Optimizer: aws compute-optimizer update-enrollment-status --status Active
- Get rightsizing recommendations: aws compute-optimizer get-ec2-instance-recommendations --query 'instanceRecommendations[].{InstanceId:instanceArn,Finding:finding,RecommendedType:recommendationOptions[0].instanceType}'

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Check VM scale sets: az vmss list --query '[].{Name:name, Capacity:sku.capacity}' -o table

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify capacity monitoring and auto-scaling configurations

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Protection against malware shall be implemented and supported by appropriate user awareness.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Enable GuardDuty Malware Protection and Inspector for vulnerability scanning.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Enable GuardDuty Malware Protection: aws guardduty update-detector --detector-id <id> --features '[{"Name":"EBS_MALWARE_PROTECTION","Status":"ENABLED"}]'
- Enable Inspector for EC2 scanning: aws inspector2 enable --resource-types EC2
- List Inspector findings: aws inspector2 list-findings --filter-criteria '{"severity":[{"comparison":"EQUALS","value":"CRITICAL"}]}'
- Enable GuardDuty S3 protection: aws guardduty update-detector --detector-id <id> --data-sources S3Logs={Enable=true}
- Check GuardDuty findings for malware: aws guardduty list-findings --detector-id <id> --finding-criteria '{"Criterion":{"type":{"Eq":["Trojan:EC2/DropPoint","Trojan:EC2/BlackholeTraffic"]}}}'

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Enable Defender for Servers: az security pricing create --name VirtualMachines --tier Standard

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify malware protection services are enabled

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Information about technical vulnerabilities of information systems in use shall be obtained, the organization's exposure to such vulnerabilities shall be evaluated and appropriate measures shall be taken.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Enable automated vulnerability scanning with Inspector and implement patching via Systems Manager.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Enable Inspector: aws inspector2 enable --resource-types EC2 ECR LAMBDA
- List critical findings: aws inspector2 list-findings --filter-criteria '{"severity":[{"comparison":"EQUALS","value":"CRITICAL"}]}' --sort-criteria '{"field":"SEVERITY","sortOrder":"DESC"}'
- Create SSM patch baseline: aws ssm create-patch-baseline --name SecurityPatchBaseline --operating-system AMAZON_LINUX_2 --approval-rules '{"PatchRules":[{"PatchFilterGroup":{"PatchFilters":[{"Key":"SEVERITY","Values":["Critical","Important"]}]},"ApproveAfterDays":3,"ComplianceLevel":"CRITICAL"}]}'
- Run patch scan: aws ssm send-command --document-name AWS-RunPatchBaseline --targets Key=instanceids,Values=<instance-id> --parameters Operation=Scan
- Check patch compliance: aws ssm list-compliance-items --resource-ids <instance-id> --resource-types ManagedInstance --filters Key=ComplianceType,Values=Patch,Type=EQUAL

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Check Defender vulnerability findings: az security assessment list --query '[?status.code!=`Healthy`].{Name:displayName, Status:status.code}'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify vulnerability scanning and remediation processes

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Configurations, including security configurations, of hardware, software, services and networks shall be established, documented, implemented, monitored and reviewed.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Use AWS Config to track and enforce configuration baselines.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Enable AWS Config: aws configservice put-configuration-recorder --configuration-recorder name=default,roleARN=<role-arn> --recording-group allSupported=true
- Start recording: aws configservice start-configuration-recorder --configuration-recorder-name default
- Deploy conformance pack: aws configservice put-conformance-pack --conformance-pack-name SecurityBaseline --template-body file://security-conformance-pack.yaml
- Check compliance: aws configservice describe-compliance-by-config-rule --query 'ComplianceByConfigRules[?Compliance.ComplianceType!=`COMPLIANT`]'
- Get configuration history: aws configservice get-resource-config-history --resource-type AWS::EC2::Instance --resource-id <instance-id>

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - List policy compliance: az policy state list --query '[?complianceState!=`Compliant`].{Resource:resourceId, Policy:policyDefinitionName}'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify configuration management and drift detection

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Information stored in information systems, devices or in any other storage media shall be deleted when no longer required.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement lifecycle policies for automatic data deletion and verify secure deletion procedures.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Set S3 lifecycle expiration: aws s3api put-bucket-lifecycle-configuration --bucket <bucket> --lifecycle-configuration '{"Rules":[{"ID":"DataRetention","Status":"Enabled","Expiration":{"Days":365},"Filter":{"Prefix":""}}]}'
- Set CloudWatch log retention: aws logs put-retention-policy --log-group-name <log-group> --retention-in-days 365
- List log groups without retention: aws logs describe-log-groups --query 'logGroups[?!retentionInDays].{Name:logGroupName}'
- Set DynamoDB TTL: aws dynamodb update-time-to-live --table-name <table> --time-to-live-specification Enabled=true,AttributeName=ttl

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Set storage lifecycle: az storage account management-policy create --account-name <account> --resource-group <rg> --policy @retention-policy.json

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify data retention and deletion policies are configured

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Data masking shall be used in accordance with the organization's topic-specific policy on access control and other related topic-specific policies, and business requirements, taking into consideration applicable legislation.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement data masking using Macie for discovery and DynamoDB/RDS features for masking.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Enable Macie: aws macie2 enable-macie
- Create classification job: aws macie2 create-classification-job --job-type ONE_TIME --s3-job-definition '{"bucketDefinitions":[{"accountId":"<account-id>","buckets":["<bucket>"]}]}' --name SensitiveDataScan
- List Macie findings: aws macie2 list-findings --finding-criteria '{"criterion":{"severity.description":{"eq":["High"]}}}'
- Create RDS snapshot for masked copy: aws rds create-db-snapshot --db-instance-identifier <db-id> --db-snapshot-identifier masked-snapshot
- Check CloudWatch Logs data protection: aws logs describe-log-groups --query 'logGroups[].{Name:logGroupName,DataProtection:dataProtectionStatus}'

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Enable dynamic data masking: az sql db data-masking-rule create --resource-group <rg> --server <server> --database <db> --schema dbo --table Users --column SSN --masking-function Default

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify data masking is implemented for sensitive data

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Data leakage prevention measures shall be applied to systems, networks and any other devices that process, store or transmit sensitive information.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement DLP using Macie, VPC endpoints, and S3 access controls.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Enable Macie for S3 DLP: aws macie2 enable-macie
- Block S3 public access at account level: aws s3control put-public-access-block --account-id <account-id> --public-access-block-configuration BlockPublicAcls=true,IgnorePublicAcls=true,BlockPublicPolicy=true,RestrictPublicBuckets=true
- Create VPC endpoint for S3: aws ec2 create-vpc-endpoint --vpc-id <vpc-id> --service-name com.amazonaws.<region>.s3 --route-table-ids <rtb-id>
- Create SCP denying data exfiltration services: aws organizations create-policy --name DenyDataExfiltration --type SERVICE_CONTROL_POLICY --content '{"Version":"2012-10-17","Statement":[{"Effect":"Deny","Action":["s3:PutBucketPolicy"],"Resource":"*","Condition":{"StringNotEquals":{"aws:PrincipalOrgID":"<org-id>"}}}]}'
- Enable CloudWatch Logs data protection: aws logs create-log-group --log-group-name /secure/application && aws logs put-data-protection-policy --log-group-identifier /secure/application --policy-document file://data-protection-policy.json

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Create DLP policy: az rest --method POST --uri /compliance/dataLossPreventionPolicies --body @dlp-policy.json

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify data leakage prevention controls are active

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Backup copies of information, software and systems shall be maintained and regularly tested in accordance with the agreed topic-specific policy on backup.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement automated backups using AWS Backup with regular testing.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Create backup plan: aws backup create-backup-plan --backup-plan '{"BackupPlanName":"DailyBackupPlan","Rules":[{"RuleName":"DailyBackup","TargetBackupVaultName":"Default","ScheduleExpression":"cron(0 5 ? * * *)","StartWindowMinutes":60,"CompletionWindowMinutes":180,"Lifecycle":{"DeleteAfterDays":90}}]}'
- Assign resources: aws backup create-backup-selection --backup-plan-id <plan-id> --backup-selection '{"SelectionName":"AllResources","IamRoleArn":"<role-arn>","Resources":["*"]}'
- List recovery points: aws backup list-recovery-points-by-backup-vault --backup-vault-name Default --query 'RecoveryPoints[].{ResourceArn:ResourceArn,CreationDate:CreationDate,Status:Status}'
- Create restore testing plan: aws backup create-restore-testing-plan --restore-testing-plan '{"RestoreTestingPlanName":"MonthlyRestoreTest","ScheduleExpression":"cron(0 8 1 * ? *)","RecoveryPointSelection":{"Algorithm":"LATEST_WITHIN_WINDOW","RecoveryPointTypes":["CONTINUOUS","SNAPSHOT"],"IncludeVaults":["*"]}}'

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Create backup policy: az backup policy create --resource-group <rg> --vault-name <vault> --name DailyPolicy --policy @backup-policy.json

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify backup policies and regular backup testing

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Information processing facilities shall be implemented with redundancy sufficient to meet availability requirements.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement multi-AZ deployments and redundancy for critical services.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Check RDS multi-AZ: aws rds describe-db-instances --query 'DBInstances[].{DB:DBInstanceIdentifier,MultiAZ:MultiAZ,Engine:Engine}'
- Verify ELB across AZs: aws elbv2 describe-load-balancers --query 'LoadBalancers[].{Name:LoadBalancerName,AZs:AvailabilityZones[].ZoneName,Scheme:Scheme}'
- Check Auto Scaling group AZ distribution: aws autoscaling describe-auto-scaling-groups --query 'AutoScalingGroups[].{Name:AutoScalingGroupName,AZs:AvailabilityZones,MinSize:MinSize}'
- Verify ElastiCache replication: aws elasticache describe-replication-groups --query 'ReplicationGroups[].{Id:ReplicationGroupId,MultiAZ:MultiAZ,Clusters:MemberClusters}'

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Check zone redundancy: az vm list --query '[].{Name:name, Zones:zones}' -o table

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify redundancy and high availability configurations

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Logs that record activities, exceptions, faults and other relevant events shall be produced, stored, protected and analysed.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Enable CloudTrail, VPC Flow Logs, and CloudWatch Logs for comprehensive audit logging.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Enable CloudTrail with log validation: aws cloudtrail create-trail --name AuditTrail --s3-bucket-name <trail-bucket> --is-multi-region-trail --enable-log-file-validation --include-global-service-events
- Start CloudTrail logging: aws cloudtrail start-logging --name AuditTrail
- Enable VPC Flow Logs: aws ec2 create-flow-logs --resource-type VPC --resource-ids <vpc-id> --traffic-type ALL --log-destination-type cloud-watch-logs --log-group-name /aws/vpc/flowlogs
- Set log retention: aws logs put-retention-policy --log-group-name /aws/vpc/flowlogs --retention-in-days 365
- Enable S3 access logging: aws s3api put-bucket-logging --bucket <bucket> --bucket-logging-status '{"LoggingEnabled":{"TargetBucket":"<log-bucket>","TargetPrefix":"s3-access-logs/"}}'

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Enable diagnostic logging: az monitor diagnostic-settings create --resource <resource-id> --name AuditLogs --logs '[{"category":"AuditEvent","enabled":true,"retentionPolicy":{"days":365,"enabled":true}}]' --workspace <workspace-id>

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify comprehensive logging is enabled across all services

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Networks, systems and applications shall be monitored for anomalous behaviour and appropriate actions taken to evaluate potential information security incidents.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Enable GuardDuty, Security Hub, and CloudWatch anomaly detection for continuous monitoring.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Enable GuardDuty: aws guardduty create-detector --enable --finding-publishing-frequency FIFTEEN_MINUTES
- Enable Security Hub: aws securityhub enable-security-hub --enable-default-standards
- Create CloudWatch anomaly detector: aws cloudwatch put-anomaly-detector --namespace AWS/EC2 --metric-name CPUUtilization --stat Average
- Create anomaly alarm: aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm --alarm-name CPUAnomaly --metric-name CPUUtilization --namespace AWS/EC2 --threshold-metric-id ad1 --comparison-operator LessThanLowerOrGreaterThanUpperThreshold --evaluation-periods 3 --alarm-actions <sns-arn> --metrics '[{"Id":"m1","MetricStat":{"Metric":{"Namespace":"AWS/EC2","MetricName":"CPUUtilization"},"Period":300,"Stat":"Average"}},{"Id":"ad1","Expression":"ANOMALY_DETECTION_BAND(m1, 2)"}]'
- List GuardDuty findings: aws guardduty list-findings --detector-id <id> --finding-criteria '{"Criterion":{"severity":{"Gte":4}}}'

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Enable Sentinel: az sentinel alert-rule list --workspace-name <ws> --resource-group <rg>

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify anomaly detection and monitoring are configured

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The clocks of information processing systems used by the organization shall be synchronized to approved time sources.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Ensure all instances use Amazon Time Sync Service or approved NTP sources.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Check instance NTP configuration via SSM: aws ssm send-command --document-name AWS-RunShellScript --targets Key=instanceids,Values=<instance-id> --parameters 'commands=["chronyc tracking"]'
- Verify CloudTrail timestamps are consistent: aws cloudtrail lookup-events --lookup-attributes AttributeKey=EventName,AttributeValue=ConsoleLogin --max-results 5 --query 'Events[].{EventTime:EventTime,EventName:EventName}'
- Check instance metadata for time sync: aws ssm send-command --document-name AWS-RunShellScript --targets Key=instanceids,Values=<instance-id> --parameters 'commands=["timedatectl status"]'
- Deploy SSM document for NTP configuration: aws ssm create-document --name ConfigureNTP --content file://ntp-config-document.json --document-type Command

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Check VM time sync: az vm run-command invoke --resource-group <rg> --name <vm> --command-id RunShellScript --scripts 'timedatectl status'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify NTP synchronization across all instances

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The use of utility programs that might be capable of overriding system and application controls shall be restricted and tightly controlled.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Restrict access to privileged tools and monitor their usage via CloudTrail and SSM.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Create IAM policy denying privileged actions: aws iam create-policy --policy-name DenyPrivilegedUtilities --policy-document '{"Version":"2012-10-17","Statement":[{"Effect":"Deny","Action":["ssm:SendCommand","ssm:StartSession"],"Resource":"*","Condition":{"StringNotEquals":{"aws:PrincipalTag/Role":"Admin"}}}]}'
- Enable SSM session logging: aws ssm update-document --name SSM-SessionManagerRunShell --content '{"schemaVersion":"1.0","description":"Session Manager Settings","sessionType":"Standard_Stream","inputs":{"cloudWatchLogGroupName":"/aws/ssm/sessions","cloudWatchEncryptionEnabled":true,"s3BucketName":"<session-log-bucket>"}}' --document-version '$LATEST'
- Monitor privileged API calls: aws cloudtrail lookup-events --lookup-attributes AttributeKey=EventName,AttributeValue=SendCommand --max-results 10
- Create EventBridge rule for privileged actions: aws events put-rule --name PrivilegedUtilityUsage --event-pattern '{"source":["aws.ssm"],"detail-type":["AWS API Call via CloudTrail"],"detail":{"eventName":["SendCommand","StartSession"]}}'

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Check privileged operations log: az monitor activity-log list --query "[?authorization.action=='Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines/runCommand/action']" --start-time 2026-01-01

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify privileged utility program usage is restricted and monitored

controls.title: Installation of software on operational systems

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Procedures and measures shall be implemented to securely manage software installation on operational systems.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Control software installation using SSM, restrict package sources, and monitor changes.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Create SSM association for software inventory: aws ssm create-association --name AWS-GatherSoftwareInventory --targets Key=instanceids,Values=* --schedule-expression 'rate(12 hours)'
- List installed software: aws ssm list-inventory-entries --instance-id <instance-id> --type-name AWS:Application
- Create Config rule for approved AMIs: aws configservice put-config-rule --config-rule file://approved-amis-rule.json
- Deploy software via SSM: aws ssm send-command --document-name AWS-RunShellScript --targets Key=tag:PatchGroup,Values=Production --parameters 'commands=["yum install -y approved-package"]'

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Check installed applications: az vm run-command invoke --resource-group <rg> --name <vm> --command-id RunShellScript --scripts 'rpm -qa'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify software installation controls and approved package management

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Networks and network devices shall be secured, managed and controlled to protect information in systems and applications.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement network security using VPC security groups, NACLs, and network firewalls.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Audit security groups for unrestricted access: aws ec2 describe-security-groups --query 'SecurityGroups[?IpPermissions[?IpRanges[?CidrIp==`0.0.0.0/0`]]].{GroupId:GroupId,GroupName:GroupName}'
- Create Config rule for restricted SSH: aws configservice put-config-rule --config-rule '{"ConfigRuleName":"restricted-ssh","Source":{"Owner":"AWS","SourceIdentifier":"INCOMING_SSH_DISABLED"}}'
- Enable VPC Flow Logs: aws ec2 create-flow-logs --resource-type VPC --resource-ids <vpc-id> --traffic-type ALL --log-destination-type cloud-watch-logs --log-group-name /aws/vpc/flowlogs
- Create network ACL rule: aws ec2 create-network-acl-entry --network-acl-id <nacl-id> --rule-number 100 --protocol tcp --port-range From=443,To=443 --cidr-block 10.0.0.0/8 --rule-action allow --ingress

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - List NSG rules: az network nsg rule list --resource-group <rg> --nsg-name <nsg> -o table

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify network security controls including security groups and NACLs

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Security mechanisms, service levels and service requirements of network services shall be identified, implemented and monitored.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Secure network services with TLS, WAF, and API Gateway controls.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Verify ALB HTTPS listeners: aws elbv2 describe-listeners --load-balancer-arn <lb-arn> --query 'Listeners[].{Protocol:Protocol,SslPolicy:SslPolicy,Port:Port}'
- Create WAF web ACL: aws wafv2 create-web-acl --name AppProtection --scope REGIONAL --default-action '{"Allow":{}}' --visibility-config '{"SampledRequestsEnabled":true,"CloudWatchMetricsEnabled":true,"MetricName":"AppProtection"}' --rules file://waf-rules.json
- Check API Gateway authentication: aws apigateway get-rest-apis --query 'items[].{Name:name,Id:id}'
- Verify CloudFront uses TLS: aws cloudfront list-distributions --query 'DistributionList.Items[].{Id:Id,ViewerProtocol:DefaultCacheBehavior.ViewerProtocolPolicy}'

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Check Application Gateway WAF: az network application-gateway waf-config show --resource-group <rg> --gateway-name <gw>

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify network service security configurations

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Groups of information services, users and information systems shall be segregated in the organization's networks.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement network segregation using separate VPCs, subnets, and security groups for different tiers.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- List VPCs and their CIDR blocks: aws ec2 describe-vpcs --query 'Vpcs[].{VpcId:VpcId,CidrBlock:CidrBlock,Tags:Tags[?Key==`Name`].Value|[0]}'
- List subnets with tier tags: aws ec2 describe-subnets --query 'Subnets[].{SubnetId:SubnetId,VpcId:VpcId,CidrBlock:CidrBlock,AZ:AvailabilityZone,Name:Tags[?Key==`Name`].Value|[0]}'
- Verify route table isolation: aws ec2 describe-route-tables --query 'RouteTables[].{RouteTableId:RouteTableId,VpcId:VpcId,Routes:Routes[].{Destination:DestinationCidrBlock,Target:GatewayId}}'
- Check for VPC peering: aws ec2 describe-vpc-peering-connections --query 'VpcPeeringConnections[].{PeeringId:VpcPeeringConnectionId,Status:Status.Code,Requester:RequesterVpcInfo.VpcId,Accepter:AccepterVpcInfo.VpcId}'

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - List virtual networks: az network vnet list --query '[].{Name:name, AddressSpace:addressSpace.addressPrefixes}' -o table

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify network segregation through VPCs, subnets, and security groups

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Access to external websites shall be managed to reduce exposure to malicious content.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement web filtering using Network Firewall and DNS filtering.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Create Network Firewall rule group: aws network-firewall create-rule-group --rule-group-name WebFiltering --type STATEFUL --capacity 100 --rule-group file://web-filter-rules.json
- Create Network Firewall: aws network-firewall create-firewall --firewall-name WebFilter --vpc-id <vpc-id> --subnet-mappings SubnetId=<subnet-id> --firewall-policy-arn <policy-arn>
- Enable Route 53 Resolver DNS Firewall: aws route53resolver create-firewall-domain-list --name BlockedDomains --domains file://blocked-domains.txt
- Associate DNS firewall with VPC: aws route53resolver associate-firewall-rule-group --firewall-rule-group-id <group-id> --vpc-id <vpc-id> --priority 100 --name WebFilterAssociation

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Create Azure Firewall policy: az network firewall policy create --name WebFilterPolicy --resource-group <rg>

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify web filtering controls are implemented

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Rules for the effective use of cryptography, including cryptographic key management, shall be defined and implemented.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement KMS for key management with rotation, and enforce encryption across services.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- List KMS keys: aws kms list-keys --query 'Keys[].KeyId'
- Check key rotation: aws kms get-key-rotation-status --key-id <key-id>
- Enable automatic key rotation: aws kms enable-key-rotation --key-id <key-id>
- Create KMS key with policy: aws kms create-key --description 'Data encryption key' --key-usage ENCRYPT_DECRYPT --origin AWS_KMS --policy file://key-policy.json
- Verify S3 bucket encryption: aws s3api get-bucket-encryption --bucket <bucket>
- Create Config rule for encrypted volumes: aws configservice put-config-rule --config-rule '{"ConfigRuleName":"encrypted-volumes","Source":{"Owner":"AWS","SourceIdentifier":"ENCRYPTED_VOLUMES"}}'

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - List Key Vault keys: az keyvault key list --vault-name <vault> --query '[].{Name:name, Enabled:attributes.enabled}'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify cryptographic controls and key management

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Rules for the secure development of software and systems shall be established and applied.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Integrate security scanning into CI/CD pipelines with automated quality gates.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- List CodePipeline pipelines: aws codepipeline list-pipelines --query 'pipelines[].{Name:name,Created:created}'
- Get pipeline details: aws codepipeline get-pipeline --name <pipeline> --query 'pipeline.stages[].{Name:name,Actions:actions[].{Name:name,Category:actionTypeId.category}}'
- Enable CodeGuru Reviewer: aws codeguru-reviewer associate-repository --repository '{"CodeCommit":{"Name":"<repo>"}}'
- Enable Inspector for container scanning: aws inspector2 enable --resource-types ECR
- Check ECR scan findings: aws ecr describe-image-scan-findings --repository-name <repo> --image-id imageTag=latest --query 'imageScanFindings.findingSeverityCounts'

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - List Azure DevOps pipelines: az pipelines list --query '[].{Name:name, QueueStatus:queueStatus}'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify secure SDLC practices are implemented in CI/CD pipelines

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Information security requirements shall be identified, specified and approved when developing or acquiring applications.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Define application security requirements and validate through automated scanning.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Store security requirements in S3: aws s3 cp application-security-requirements.pdf s3://<policy-bucket>/security-requirements/
- Enable WAF for application protection: aws wafv2 create-web-acl --name AppSecRequirements --scope REGIONAL --default-action '{"Allow":{}}' --visibility-config '{"SampledRequestsEnabled":true,"CloudWatchMetricsEnabled":true,"MetricName":"AppSecReqs"}' --rules file://appsec-rules.json
- Enable Inspector for Lambda scanning: aws inspector2 enable --resource-types LAMBDA
- Check Security Hub application findings: aws securityhub get-findings --filters '{"ProductName":[{"Value":"Inspector","Comparison":"EQUALS"}],"SeverityLabel":[{"Value":"CRITICAL","Comparison":"EQUALS"}]}'

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Check application security with Defender: az security assessment list --query '[?contains(displayName,`application`)].{Name:displayName, Status:status.code}'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify application security requirements are defined and enforced

controls.title: Secure system architecture and engineering principles

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Principles for engineering secure systems shall be established, documented, maintained and applied to any information system development activities.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Apply Well-Architected Framework principles and enforce security best practices.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Run Well-Architected review: aws wellarchitected create-workload --workload-name ProductionWorkload --lenses wellarchitected --environment PRODUCTION --aws-regions us-east-1 --review-owner security-team@example.com
- Deploy Security Hub best practices standard: aws securityhub batch-enable-standards --standards-subscription-requests StandardsArn=arn:aws:securityhub:::standards/aws-foundational-security-best-practices/v/1.0.0
- Enable Trusted Advisor checks: aws support describe-trusted-advisor-checks --language en --query 'checks[?category==`security`].{Name:name,Id:id}'
- Check Config conformance pack: aws configservice describe-conformance-pack-compliance --conformance-pack-name SecurityBaseline

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Run Azure Advisor security recommendations: az advisor recommendation list --category Security

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify secure architecture principles are applied to infrastructure

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Secure coding principles shall be applied to software development.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement automated code review and static analysis in CI/CD pipelines.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Associate CodeGuru Reviewer: aws codeguru-reviewer associate-repository --repository '{"CodeCommit":{"Name":"<repo>"}}'
- List CodeGuru recommendations: aws codeguru-reviewer list-recommendations --repository-association-arn <arn> --query 'RecommendationSummaries[].{FilePath:FilePath,Description:Description,Severity:Severity}'
- Create CodeBuild project with security scanning: aws codebuild create-project --name SecureCodeScan --source '{"type":"CODECOMMIT","location":"<repo-url>"}' --artifacts '{"type":"NO_ARTIFACTS"}' --environment '{"type":"LINUX_CONTAINER","computeType":"BUILD_GENERAL1_SMALL","image":"aws/codebuild/amazonlinux2-x86_64-standard:4.0"}'
- Check ECR image scan results: aws ecr describe-image-scan-findings --repository-name <repo> --image-id imageTag=latest

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Check Azure DevOps code analysis: az pipelines runs list --query '[].{Name:pipeline.name, Result:result}'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify secure coding practices and automated code analysis

controls.title: Security testing in development and acceptance

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Security testing processes shall be defined and implemented in the development life cycle.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Integrate security testing stages into CI/CD pipelines for continuous validation.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Add security test stage to pipeline: aws codepipeline update-pipeline --pipeline file://pipeline-with-security-testing.json
- Enable Inspector for continuous scanning: aws inspector2 enable --resource-types EC2 ECR LAMBDA
- Run on-demand Inspector scan: aws inspector2 create-findings-report --report-format CSV --s3-destination '{"bucketName":"<report-bucket>","keyPrefix":"security-reports/","kmsKeyArn":"<kms-arn>"}'
- Check CodeBuild test reports: aws codebuild list-report-groups --query 'reportGroups'
- Verify test reports: aws codebuild list-reports-for-report-group --report-group-arn <arn> --query 'reports'

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Check test results: az pipelines runs list --query '[?result==`succeeded`].{Name:pipeline.name, Result:result}'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify security testing is integrated into development and acceptance processes

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The organization shall direct, monitor and review the activities related to outsourced system development.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement strict access controls and monitoring for third-party development access.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Create cross-account role for vendor: aws iam create-role --role-name VendorDevelopmentAccess --assume-role-policy-document '{"Version":"2012-10-17","Statement":[{"Effect":"Allow","Principal":{"AWS":"arn:aws:iam::<vendor-account>:root"},"Action":"sts:AssumeRole","Condition":{"StringEquals":{"sts:ExternalId":"<external-id>"},"Bool":{"aws:MultiFactorAuthPresent":"true"}}}]}'
- Add permission boundary: aws iam put-role-permissions-boundary --role-name VendorDevelopmentAccess --permissions-boundary arn:aws:iam::<account>:policy/VendorBoundary
- Enable CloudTrail for vendor monitoring: aws cloudtrail put-event-selectors --trail-name AuditTrail --event-selectors '[{"ReadWriteType":"All","IncludeManagementEvents":true}]'
- Create CloudWatch alarm for vendor activity: aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm --alarm-name VendorActivity --metric-name VendorAPICallCount --namespace Security --threshold 100 --comparison-operator GreaterThanThreshold --evaluation-periods 1 --period 3600 --statistic Sum --alarm-actions <sns-arn>

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Create guest user for vendor: az ad user create --display-name 'Vendor Developer' --user-principal-name vendor@external.com --password <temp-password>

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify outsourced development access controls and monitoring

controls.title: Separation of development, test and production environments

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Development, testing and production environments shall be separated and secured.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement environment separation using AWS Organizations with separate accounts or VPCs.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- List organizational accounts: aws organizations list-accounts --query 'Accounts[].{Id:Id,Name:Name,Email:Email,Status:Status}'
- List OUs: aws organizations list-organizational-units-for-parent --parent-id <root-id> --query 'OrganizationalUnits[].{Id:Id,Name:Name}'
- Verify separate VPCs: aws ec2 describe-vpcs --query 'Vpcs[].{VpcId:VpcId,CidrBlock:CidrBlock,Tags:Tags[?Key==`Environment`].Value|[0]}'
- Create SCP preventing production changes from dev: aws organizations create-policy --name DenyDevToProd --type SERVICE_CONTROL_POLICY --content file://dev-prod-separation-scp.json
- Tag resources with environment: aws ec2 create-tags --resources <resource-id> --tags Key=Environment,Value=Production

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - List subscriptions for environment separation: az account list --query '[].{Name:name, Id:id, State:state}'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify environment separation through accounts or VPCs

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Changes to information processing facilities and information systems shall be subject to change management procedures.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Implement change management using Infrastructure as Code with approval workflows.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- List CloudFormation stacks: aws cloudformation list-stacks --stack-status-filter CREATE_COMPLETE UPDATE_COMPLETE --query 'StackSummaries[].{Name:StackName,Status:StackStatus,LastUpdated:LastUpdatedTime}'
- Check CloudFormation drift: aws cloudformation detect-stack-drift --stack-name <stack-name>
- Get drift results: aws cloudformation describe-stack-drift-detection-status --stack-drift-detection-id <detection-id>
- Create CodePipeline approval stage: aws codepipeline update-pipeline --pipeline file://pipeline-with-approval.json
- Enable Config change tracking: aws configservice put-config-rule --config-rule '{"ConfigRuleName":"cloudformation-stack-drift-detection-check","Source":{"Owner":"AWS","SourceIdentifier":"CLOUDFORMATION_STACK_DRIFT_DETECTION_CHECK"}}'

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - List deployment operations: az deployment group list --resource-group <rg> --query '[].{Name:name, Timestamp:timestamp}'

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify change management processes and approval workflows

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Test information shall be appropriately selected, protected and managed.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Ensure test environments do not contain production data, and sanitize any test data.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Enable Macie to scan test buckets: aws macie2 create-classification-job --job-type ONE_TIME --s3-job-definition '{"bucketDefinitions":[{"accountId":"<account-id>","buckets":["<test-bucket>"]}]}' --name TestDataScan
- Tag test resources: aws ec2 create-tags --resources <resource-id> --tags Key=Environment,Value=Test Key=DataClassification,Value=Sanitized
- Create Config rule for test environment tags: aws configservice put-config-rule --config-rule file://test-environment-tags.json
- Verify RDS test instances use sanitized snapshots: aws rds describe-db-instances --query 'DBInstances[?TagList[?Key==`Environment` && Value==`Test`]].{DB:DBInstanceIdentifier,Tags:TagList}'

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Tag test resources: az tag create --resource-id <id> --tags Environment=Test DataClassification=Sanitized

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify test data is sanitized and protected

controls.title: Protection of information systems during audit testing

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Audit tests and other assurance activities involving assessment of operational systems shall be planned and agreed between the tester and appropriate management.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Create time-limited read-only access for auditors with full activity logging.

parts.name: remediation-aws
parts.prose:
- Create auditor role with read-only access: aws iam create-role --role-name AuditorReadOnly --assume-role-policy-document file://auditor-trust.json --max-session-duration 3600
- Attach SecurityAudit policy: aws iam attach-role-policy --role-name AuditorReadOnly --policy-arn arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/SecurityAudit
- Attach ViewOnlyAccess policy: aws iam attach-role-policy --role-name AuditorReadOnly --policy-arn arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/job-function/ViewOnlyAccess
- Add permission boundary: aws iam put-role-permissions-boundary --role-name AuditorReadOnly --permissions-boundary arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/ReadOnlyAccess
- Monitor auditor activity: aws cloudtrail lookup-events --lookup-attributes AttributeKey=Username,AttributeValue=AuditorReadOnly --max-results 20

parts.name: remediation-azure
parts.prose: - Assign Reader role: az role assignment create --assignee <auditor-id> --role Reader --scope /subscriptions/<sub-id>

parts.name: assessment-objective
parts.prose: Verify audit testing controls and read-only access for auditors
